reviews.review_msg: I'm honestly not really sure why the other reviews are so bad. I thought Janice was a decent prof, she was approachable and kind when asking questions. I found her lectures to be informative. I didn't do the greatest on the midterm, I don't think many people did. I did decent on a zine project we did and have yet to see the final.

The content of this course is interesting but she I found that she overcomplicates it. Definitely put a lot of effort into the zine project because she marks that more leniently than the exams. Her exams are really long but she gives you a good outline before

I had Dr. Jeong for HIST 151 and had a very different experience from many of the negative reviews. She's kind, approachable, and very generous with her time, being available after class or in the office. The workload is reading-heavy, but expectations are clear, and any feedback requested is genuine. If you engage, she wants you to succeed.

Prof Jeong is super sweet, and really tries to get to know her students. She set plenty of time aside to discuss grades, and is extremely approachable. The grades are a bit inflated, which makes it hard to get an A+, so if you care a lot about your GPA, maybe skip past this course. Super interesting material.

Dr Marriott's enthusiasm for Middle Eastern history  is infectious and he varied his lecture format to keep  it interesting.  The quizzes might seem hard at first but keep trying - they are the best prep for the final.  He is very helpful with the research paper, he really wants you to do well.  I would definitely take another course with him.

The teacher does not provide you with any powerpoints or any notes at all, and his teaching comes straight out of "A History of the Modern Middle East" textbook. The tests are tough, so prepare yourself to know exact dates, names, and places on a map. I would not recommend this professor as he literally reads the textbook to you every class.
Do not take your GPA will go down because of this class.

His lectures have little writing on them, barely enough time do type out notes and does not record or post lecture videos. The level of work required in this course is way beyond what it should be for a 1st year course. The exams are written and there is a paper, I would not recommend this professor or course especially if its an elective.

He is a very understanding/nice prof and accommodating for extensions. He really cares for your learning, and will give the time to help his students. He only puts important terms on his slides, so be prepared to take lots of notes on each term. His lectures can get content heavy. Course included midterm, one term paper, and take home 24hr final.

Only 3 assignments - midterm, final, and term paper. I enjoyed the freedom of topic for the paper but the midterm and final were far too advanced for a 1st year course.  This is the most difficult 1st year course I have taken. Lots of reading. Upper level students with history experience may be better suited as Dr. Rezamand does not hold back.

Decent prof (albeit grades rather toughly) with an acceptable workload for a freshmen year SFU History course. Transparent & playful with regards to the dissemination of his lectures and to what he is teaching. Preferred that exams were based more on the slides than the readings as during some weeks, there was an inordinate amount to process.

Professor Rezamand was a nice professor, I thought his HIST 151 course was an interesting exploration of the 'Modern Middle East'.Tutorial was held by him and was largely a discussion, so be prepared with the readings ahead of time. The outline was fairly clear, but he is very easy to talk to, so do try and approach him with questions if needed.

i will admit readings are heavy at times but he does a great job of explaining concepts. when he teaches topics that may still affect people (ie palestine) he does so in a respectful manner. very kind and wants his students to do well. respects other perspectives and engages participation. i wish all the best for this prof and appreciate his help

The reading you do barely connect with lectures, he goes on tangents and speaks fast so you miss a lot of the information. His tutorials have bad structure and he leaves the room when you talk in groups. His exams are unreasonable in 50 mins he wants a 5 page paper with good grammar, names and dates that covers 150 years plus two other paragraphs

Not a good 1st year prof. Assigns at least 60 pages of reading a week, which wouldn't be terrible if his lectures focused on them. He often goes off on tangents, leaving students feeling lost and confused. His tutorials aren't helpful, just discussions. A very tough marker and his grades aren't subject to negotiation (against school policy).
I found her a little bit opinionated for the content and a little dry. she should speak louder too.

Julian is really passionate about history and he's a really nice approachable guy. It's evident that he really cares about his students and wants them to do well. One of the textbooks for the class is a comic book which was really interesting! The only thing I didn't like was his use of Tophat. Overall, I loved the class and would recommend it!

Awesome prof, very knowledgeable and if you ask for help he is always there! Would recommend for any class. Quite a few things that need to be done during the semester but its all straightforward and doable.
Goated prof. Not too hard but very enjoyable class.

A very interesting class with very engaging lectures. Exams were based off of material in 5 or so lectures that weren't recorded, and we were given lots of pointers for the essay. Expectations are fair, bordering on easy. An average of 50 pages of reading were assigned per week.

Currently taking 151 with Paul this semester. He gives very clear, detailed expectations on assignment criteria. His lectures are super engaging and very interesting. He's a super smart guy, and he repeats key terms a lot to ensure students are locking in the important info. Would def recommend taking a course with Paul, pretty goated prof tbh

sedra is an amazing lecturer but be prepared to cover a lot of information very quickly! extremely knowledgable on the topic of the middle east and a respectable guy. if you take hist151 i would recommend doing some prior research as he will assume you know what he is talking about!!

textbook, get ur textbook beforehand. will literally save ur ass in this class i swear. super nice, funny, and accessible after class for any questions. TA was hot, and nice lmfao explained stuff well, participate! attendance in tutorials mattered. midterm/final was text-heavy, read beforehand. answering questions in paragraphs+ gives a word limit.
He is very excited and enthusiastic about the topic which I greatly appreciate. His tutorials are clear and it is so far and he is very friendly.
